sources.label: Android KeyGenParameterSpec Builder
sources.locator: API 23+: PURPOSE_SIGN with DIGEST_SHA256 produces a self-signed EC certificate; DIGEST_NONE is also authorized because TLS stacks typically sign an already-digested input

sources.label: Android KeyStore certificate and alias lifecycle
sources.locator: getCertificate returns the leaf certificate for fingerprinting and deleteEntry removes the Lounge-scoped alias

sources.label: Android KeyManagerFactory
sources.locator: init(KeyStore, password) and getKeyManagers load the generated identity material for secure socket negotiation

sources.label: Android X509KeyManager
sources.locator: chooseServerAlias selects the exact Lounge alias used to authenticate the TLS server side

sources.label: Android SSLContext
sources.locator: TLSv1.3 is available on API 29+ and init installs explicit KeyManager and TrustManager arrays

sources.label: Android Certificate encoding
sources.locator: getEncoded returns the single ASN.1 DER encoding used for an X.509 leaf certificate fingerprint

sources.label: Android MessageDigest
sources.locator: SHA-256 hashes the leaf DER and isEqual compares the QR and peer digests without content-dependent timing

sources.label: Android X509TrustManager API
sources.locator: checkServerTrusted receives the peer X.509 chain and throws CertificateException when the leaf fingerprint is not trusted

candidates.name: mDNS + Local WebSocket / Secure Channel
candidates.exactRoute: DNS-SD is optional discovery; QR carries a direct host hint and SHA-256 leaf-certificate fingerprint; iOS Network.framework and Android NsdManager plus JSSE exchange the same bounded length-prefixed application frames over TLS 1.3 with no plaintext fallback
candidates.rationale: This is the only route that keeps one cross-platform wire, standard TLS, and QR recovery without a third-party transport SDK, but application-controlled telemetry remains Not run until native-build artifact evidence exists.

ios.apiRoute: NWListener and NWConnection with TLS options, Bonjour advertisement, and leaf-certificate fingerprint verification

android.apiRoute: NsdManager DNS-SD; Lounge-scoped EC P-256 AndroidKeyStore identity with DIGEST_SHA256 and DIGEST_NONE; KeyStore certificate access and deletion; KeyManagerFactory material loading; X509KeyManager server-alias selection; SSLContext with X509TrustManager leaf-DER SHA-256 pinning; SSLServerSocket and SSLSocket restricted to TLS 1.3

officialSourceAndVersion.reason: Repository versions and Expo platform SDK baseline are exact; the route uses only public Apple and Android system-framework locators.

crossPlatformRoute.reason: Both platforms advertise or resolve DNS-SD and exchange the same bounded framed protocol over interoperable TLS sockets; QR bypasses discovery.

expoAndNewArchitecture.reason: The route is a local Swift and Kotlin Expo module in a Development Build; Expo Modules support the New Architecture by default.

standardSecureChannel.reason: Apple Network.framework supplies the local TLS identity route; Android API 29+ supplies TLS 1.3 while AndroidKeyStore creates a Lounge-scoped EC signing key with self-sign and TLS digests, KeyStore exposes its leaf and deletion, KeyManagerFactory loads the material, X509KeyManager fixes the server alias, SSLContext installs the managers, and X509TrustManager rejects a mismatched SHA-256 of the peer leaf DER during the handshake.

licenseAndMaintenance.reason: No third-party transport package, native artifact, or downloader is added; Apple and Android public system APIs are compiled by the pinned Expo platform toolchain.

applicationControlledTelemetry.reason: The route adds no third-party transport SDK, but no iOS or Android native build artifact exists yet for dependency, symbol, configuration, and endpoint verification.

topologyAndDiscovery.reason: One Host listener relays to at most five Guests; DNS-SD is optional and the QR direct host hint remains the recovery route when discovery is disabled.

candidates.name: WebRTC DataChannel + QR Signaling
candidates.exactRoute: react-native-webrtc 124.0.7 with QR-carried SDP and ICE host candidates, one Host RTCPeerConnection per Guest, and DataChannel application frames
candidates.rationale: The cross-platform wire is viable, but floating native artifacts, unproven Expo compatibility, unavailable peer fingerprint binding, and unknown telemetry prevent selection.

officialSourceAndVersion.reason: The JavaScript package tag and source commit are exact, but both native Jitsi artifact declarations are ranges rather than exact lock resolutions.

crossPlatformRoute.reason: Both platforms use the same WebRTC DataChannel wire route and QR can carry the out-of-band signaling description.

expoAndNewArchitecture.reason: The official Expo note requires a Development Client and out-of-tree plugin but does not establish exact Expo SDK 57 and React Native 0.86 compatibility.

standardSecureChannel.reason: DTLS protects DataChannel traffic, but the reviewed React Native API route does not expose a peer certificate fingerprint that can be bound to the QR proof.

licenseAndMaintenance.reason: The package is maintained and MIT-licensed, but floating iOS and Android Jitsi native artifact resolutions violate reproducible provenance.

applicationControlledTelemetry.reason: The transitive Jitsi native artifacts and their application-controlled collection configuration are not fixed or evidenced by the reviewed route.

topologyAndDiscovery.reason: A Host can maintain one DataChannel per Guest and QR signaling removes the need for a central signaling service or discovery service.

candidates.name: Platform Adapter (Multipeer / Nearby)
candidates.exactRoute: Google Nearby Connections on Android and the google/nearby Swift package on iOS using P2P_STAR; Apple Multipeer Connectivity is not mixed into this route
candidates.rationale: Application-controlled telemetry, exact package provenance, peer fingerprint binding, and QR discovery recovery do not meet the static gate.

officialSourceAndVersion.reason: Official setup routes exist for Android and Swift, but the reviewed setup sources do not fix a shared exact release, source commit, and both lock resolutions.

crossPlatformRoute.reason: Google documents Nearby Connections and a Swift package route, so this candidate does not combine unrelated Apple Multipeer and Android protocols.

expoAndNewArchitecture.reason: No exact Expo SDK 57 and React Native 0.86 local-module build route with fixed Android and Swift packages is evidenced.

standardSecureChannel.reason: Google states that connections are encrypted and symmetrically authenticated, but the reviewed API does not provide the peer key or certificate fingerprint required by the QR binding contract.

licenseAndMaintenance.reason: Both native package versions, lock resolutions, source commit, and native artifact provenance remain unresolved.

applicationControlledTelemetry.reason: The SDK collects performance and device information, and the official control is an end-user Google Usage and diagnostics setting rather than an application API or build configuration.

topologyAndDiscovery.reason: P2P_STAR is available, but a QR direct host hint cannot bypass Nearby endpoint advertising and discovery under the reviewed API route.

candidates.name: BLE Custom Protocol (comparison only)
candidates.exactRoute: A common custom GATT service over iOS Core Bluetooth and Android BLE with one Host peripheral and up to five Guest centrals; QR carries service identity but BLE discovery remains required
candidates.rationale: Cross-platform GATT is possible, but a custom security protocol and mandatory discovery fail the route; telemetry remains Not run without native build artifacts.

ios.apiRoute: CBPeripheralManager Host and CBCentralManager Guest over a custom GATT service

android.apiRoute: BluetoothGattServer Host and BluetoothGatt Guest over the same custom GATT service

officialSourceAndVersion.reason: The route uses public iOS and Android system BLE APIs under the exact Expo platform SDK baseline.

crossPlatformRoute.reason: Both platforms can implement the same application-defined GATT service and characteristic framing.

expoAndNewArchitecture.reason: The comparison route can be isolated in a local Swift and Kotlin Expo module supported by the New Architecture.

standardSecureChannel.reason: The common GATT route does not provide a standard end-to-end secure channel with a peer certificate fingerprint; meeting the contract would require a custom security protocol.

licenseAndMaintenance.reason: No third-party transport package, native artifact, or downloader is added; both BLE APIs are maintained system frameworks.

applicationControlledTelemetry.reason: No third-party transport SDK is planned, but no iOS or Android native build artifact exists for dependency, symbol, configuration, and endpoint verification.

topologyAndDiscovery.reason: BLE service discovery remains required after QR scanning, so the candidate cannot provide the required QR direct hint recovery when discovery is disabled.
